visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.purpose: Short-term leisure and tourism visits to Myanmar.
visa_types.notes: Available as e-Visa (USD 50, 28 days, valid 90 days from issuance) for approximately 100 nationalities. Also available via Visa on Arrival at Yangon, Mandalay, and Naypyidaw International Airports for China, India, Japan, South Korea, Macau SAR, and Hong Kong SAR nationals (pilot programs). Embassy/consulate tourist visa fee is USD 40. Entry at designated international airports and Kawthaung land border.

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: Visits for business meetings, negotiations, and commercial activities in Myanmar.
visa_types.notes: Available as e-Visa (USD 70) for 54 nationalities; requires invitation from registered Myanmar company or Chamber of Commerce. Also available as Visa on Arrival (USD 50, 70 days) at Yangon, Mandalay, and Naypyidaw International Airports for eligible nationalities. Multiple-entry options available at embassy (3 months USD 200, 6 months USD 400, 1 year USD 600).

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Short-term transit through Myanmar en route to another destination.
visa_types.notes: Allows 24-hour transit. Available on arrival at Yangon, Mandalay, and Naypyidaw International Airports for eligible nationalities including most Western countries, ASEAN, and major Asian nations.

visa_types.name: Employment Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry for foreign nationals taking up employment in Myanmar.
visa_types.notes: Initial single-entry Employment Visa (USD 50, 70 days) is obtained from Myanmar embassy abroad. After arrival, a Stay Permit must be obtained from the Ministry of Immigration. Multiple-entry versions available for extended validity. Work permit from Ministry of Labour is required separately.

visa_types.name: Education Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry for foreign nationals enrolled in educational or training programs in Myanmar.
visa_types.notes: Single-entry Education Visa (USD 50, 90 days). Multiple-entry options: 3 months USD 200, 6 months USD 400, 1 year USD 600. Requires acceptance letter from an accredited Myanmar educational institution. Extensions possible with appropriate documentation.

visa_types.name: Social Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry for foreign nationals visiting family members or for social/humanitarian purposes in Myanmar.
visa_types.notes: Single-entry Social Visa (USD 50, 70 days). Multiple-entry options available: 3 months USD 200, 6 months USD 300, 1 year USD 450. Covers family visits and social activities.

visa_types.name: Religious Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry for foreign nationals engaging in religious activities, missionary work, or pilgrimages in Myanmar.
visa_types.notes: Single-entry Religious Visa (USD 50, 70 days). Multiple-entry options: 3 months USD 200, 6 months USD 300, 1 year USD 450. Requires documentation from a recognized religious organization.

visa_types.name: Journalist Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry for foreign journalists and media personnel covering events in Myanmar.
visa_types.notes: Single-entry Journalist Visa (USD 40, 28 days). Requires press credentials and assignment documentation. Must apply at Myanmar embassy abroad. Note: Myanmar has heavily restricted press access since the February 2021 coup; approvals are rare in practice.

visa_types.name: Workshop / Seminar / Meeting / Research Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry for foreign nationals attending or presenting at official workshops, seminars, conferences, or conducting research in Myanmar.
visa_types.notes: Single-entry, 28 days, USD 40. Available via embassy and also as Visa on Arrival at Yangon, Mandalay, Naypyidaw International Airports for eligible nationalities. Research visa holders may apply for extensions. Invitation/approval letter from Myanmar institution or government agency required.

visa_types.name: Crew Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry for air crew and maritime crew members transiting or working in Myanmar.
visa_types.notes: Single-entry Crew Visa (USD 40, 28 days). Available on arrival at Yangon, Mandalay, Naypyidaw International Airports for eligible nationalities. Multiple-entry versions available from embassies (up to 1 year, USD 600). Requires crew member documentation.

visa_types.name: Official / Diplomatic Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry for holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports traveling on government business.
visa_types.notes: Diplomatic/Courtesy Visa is issued free of charge. Official Visa for non-diplomatic government travelers: single-entry USD 50, multiple-entry options USD 200-600. Separate from bilateral diplomatic visa exemption agreements. Duration determined case-by-case.

visa_free.notes: Ordinary passport holders: 14 days. Diplomatic/Official: 14 days each. ASEAN member. Entry via Yangon, Mandalay, Naypyidaw International Airports.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Tourist Visa on Arrival. Fee: USD 50. Originally launched 30 August 2023 (first one-year pilot). Extended for a second one-year pilot period from 30 August 2025 through 29 August 2026. Currently active as of June 2026. No extensions of stay permitted. Entry at international airports only.

Tourist Visa on Arrival. Fee: USD 50. Originally launched 30 August 2023 (first one-year pilot). Extended for a second one-year pilot period from 30 August 2025 through 29 August 2026. Currently active as of June 2026. No extensions of stay permitted.

Tourist Visa on Arrival. Fee: USD 50. Originally launched 11 March 2024 (first one-year pilot). Extended for a second one-year period from 11 March 2025 through 10 March 2026. PILOT EXPIRED 10 MARCH 2026. No renewal confirmed as of June 2026 — Myanmar Consulate HK announcement page and web search show no extension announced. Entry retained in record per preservation rule; must be re-checked for renewal announcement.

Tourist Visa on Arrival. Fee: USD 50. Originally launched 21 October 2024 (first one-year pilot). Renewed for a second one-year period from 21 October 2025 through 20 October 2026. Currently active as of June 2026. No extensions of stay permitted.

Tourist Visa on Arrival. Fee: USD 50. Originally launched 21 October 2024 (first one-year pilot). Renewed for a second one-year period from 21 October 2025 through 20 October 2026. Currently active as of June 2026. No extensions of stay permitted.

Australia, Austria, Belgium, Brazil, Brunei, Bulgaria, Cambodia, Canada, China, Croatia, Czech, Cyprus, Denmark, DPRK, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, India, Indonesia, Ireland, Israel, Italy, Japan, Korea, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malaysia, Malta, Nepal, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Philippines, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Russia, Singapore, Slovakia, Slovenia, South Africa, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Thailand, Ukraine, UK, USA, Vietnam, Taiwan eligible for Business (70 days, USD 50), Workshop/Seminar (28 days, USD 40), Transit (24 hours, USD 20), Crew (28 days, USD 40) visas on arrival. Entry at Yangon, Mandalay, Naypyidaw International Airports.

e_visa.notes: Albania, Algeria, Argentina, Australia, Austria, Bangladesh, Belarus, Belgium, Bhutan, Bolivia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Brazil, Brunei, Bulgaria, Cambodia, Cameroon, Canada, Chile, China, Colombia, Costa Rica, Cote d'Ivoire, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Ecuador, Egypt, Eritrea, Estonia, Fiji, Finland, France, Georgia, Germany, Ghana, Greece, Guatemala, Guinea, Hungary, Iceland, India, Indonesia, Ireland, Israel, Italy, Jamaica, Japan, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kenya, South Korea, North Korea, Kuwait, Kyrgyzstan, Laos, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malaysia, Maldives, Malta, Mauritius, Mexico, Monaco, Mongolia, Morocco, Nepal, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Pakistan, Panama, Peru, Philippines, Poland, Portugal, Qatar, Romania, Russia, Saudi Arabia, Serbia, Singapore, Slovakia, Slovenia, South Africa, Spain, Sri Lanka, Sweden, Switzerland, Thailand, Turkey, Uganda, Ukraine, United Kingdom, USA, Uruguay, Uzbekistan, Venezuela, Vietnam. Fee: USD 50. Valid 90 days from issuance; single entry; stay 28 days from arrival; processing minimum 3 working days. Entry at Yangon, Mandalay, Naypyidaw International Airports and Kawthaung International Land Border.

Australia, Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Brazil, Brunei, Cambodia, Canada, China, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, India, Indonesia, Ireland, Israel, Italy, Japan, North Korea, South Korea, Laos, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malaysia, Malta, Nepal, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Philippines, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Russian Federation, Singapore, Slovakia, Slovenia, South Africa, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Thailand, Ukraine, United Kingdom, United States, Vietnam. Fee: USD 70. Invitation from registered Myanmar company or Chamber of Commerce required. Processing minimum 3 working days.

visa_policy.notes: Visa-free for ordinary passport holders applies to 9 ASEAN members (Brunei 14d, Cambodia 14d, Indonesia 14d, Laos 14d, Malaysia 14d pilot, Philippines 14d, Singapore 30d, Thailand 14d, Vietnam 14d). Most other bilateral agreements cover diplomatic and official/service passport holders only. Active VOA pilots as of June 2026: China/India from 30 Aug 2025 through 29 Aug 2026; Japan/Korea/Macau from 21 Oct 2025 through 20 Oct 2026. Hong Kong VOA pilot EXPIRED 10 March 2026 - no renewal confirmed as of June 2026. Myanmar's political situation (military government since February 2021 coup) may affect practical application of these policies.

cbi.notes: No official citizenship-by-investment program found on any Myanmar government website. Myanmar's citizenship law (1982 Citizenship Law) does not provide for investment-based citizenship. No official announcement of such a program was found.

gaps: The evisa.moip.gov.mm portal pages rendered blank (likely JavaScript-heavy SPA) — e-visa country list was sourced from official Myanmar Consulate Hong Kong (myanmarconsulatehk.org) which mirrors the official evisa.moip.gov.mm notice.
The exact current e-visa tourist and business eligible countries may have changed since the consulate page data was last updated — the official portal evisa.moip.gov.mm could not be read directly.

No official page was found for RBI (residency by investment) or fast-track/skilled worker/digital nomad visa scheme — absence is consistent with Myanmar's current political and regulatory environment.

Japan/Korea/Macau VOA: The second pilot is confirmed as starting 21 October 2025 via tourism.gov.mm/en/information-pages/visa-on-arrival; end date of 20 October 2026 is inferred as one-year from start — a dedicated renewal announcement page was not found.

Hong Kong VOA pilot expired 10 March 2026. No renewal announcement found as of June 2026 via Myanmar Consulate HK website or web search. Entry retained in visa_on_arrival with expiry flag per preservation rule. Must be re-checked when official renewal is announced.

Serbia conditional_access entry retained from prior record (mofa.gov.mm October 2025 PDF) but not found in Embassy of Myanmar Riyadh bilateral table on June 2026 re-fetch (table lists 23 countries without Serbia). Serbia may have been omitted from the Riyadh table, or the bilateral agreement may have lapsed; kept pending direct MFA confirmation.

No official Myanmar government source was found establishing conditional entry rules based on holding a third-country visa, residence permit, or permanent residence — Myanmar's entry system is based solely on nationality and passport type.
Taiwan/Chinese Taipei is listed as eligible for Business/Workshop/Transit VOA; its status as a separate non-state entity means it has no bilateral treaty but is treated administratively.
Colombia and Sri Lanka bilateral exemption effective dates not available; only stay durations sourced from Embassy of Myanmar Riyadh bilateral table.
Myanmar does not offer digital nomad, retirement, investment, working holiday, or medical visa categories — these are absent from all official sources reviewed.

Malaysia ordinary passport visa-free pilot: exact end date not confirmed from official Myanmar source (Myanmar Consulate HK announcement references start from October 2025 as one-year pilot; approximate end approximately September/October 2026).

Russia ordinary passport: Riyadh embassy table (June 2026) lists Russia bilateral agreement with 90 days for both passport types, but no ordinary passport column value was explicitly confirmed. Russia listed in Riyadh table; kept at diplomatic/service only per prior record until direct confirmation of ordinary passport terms is available.

visa_free.name: Vietnam
visa_free.notes: ASEAN member. Ordinary passport: 30 days (per official MFA PDF dated 9 Oct 2025). This is Vietnamese policy toward Myanmar passport holders (outbound); inbound is 14 days for Vietnamese passports visiting Myanmar.

outbound_official.notes: Source is the official Myanmar Ministry of Foreign Affairs PDF 'List of Countries Having Bilateral Visa Exemption Agreements with Myanmar (As of 9 October 2025)', linked from mofa.gov.mm/consular-information/visa-free-travel/. This PDF lists 22 bilateral agreements total; only 9 ASEAN states extend visa-free access to Myanmar ORDINARY passport holders. All other bilateral agreements (Bangladesh, Belarus, Brazil, China, India, Japan, Korea ROK, Korea DPRK, Mongolia, Morocco, Nepal, Russia, Serbia) cover Diplomatic and/or Official/Service passport holders only — ordinary passport holders are not covered. Vietnam outbound shown as 30 days in this MFA PDF (this reflects Vietnamese policy toward Myanmar ordinary passports); inbound to Myanmar from Vietnam is 14 days per bilateral table.
